---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Qingyang Du"
date: "2026-02-20"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
###############################################################################
#                         DATA CLEANING SCRIPT (v3)
#
# Project:  Treatment Response to HAART and Hard Drug Use
# Data:     Multicenter AIDS Cohort Study (MACS) — hiv_6624_final.csv
# Codebook: codebook_6624_hiv.pdf
#
# Updates from investigator + professor feedback:
#   - Race: Non-Hispanic White vs. Other
#   - Education: 4-yr college+ vs. Below
#   - Smoking: Current smoker vs. Non/Former
#   - Confounders per investigator: age, race, education, BMI, smoking
#   - Income excluded from adjustment (education used for SES)
#   - VLOAD log-transformed as log10(VLOAD) — no +1 offset (no zeros exist)
#   - Adherence mediation: compare adjusted model with vs. without adherence
###############################################################################


# =============================================================================
# 0.  LOAD PACKAGES
# =============================================================================

library(tidyverse)


# =============================================================================
# 1.  IMPORT RAW DATA
# =============================================================================

hiv_raw <- read.csv("hiv_6624_final.csv", na.strings = c("", "NA", " "))

# Drop the unnamed row-index column from the CSV export
hiv_raw <- hiv_raw %>% select(-1)

# Quick inspection
str(hiv_raw)
dim(hiv_raw)       # Expect 3935 rows x 33 columns
head(hiv_raw, 10)
length(unique(hiv_raw$newid))
table(hiv_raw$years, useNA = "always")


# =============================================================================
# 2.  RECODE VARIABLES
# =============================================================================

hiv_clean <- hiv_raw


# ---------- 2.1  Comorbidity variables (multi-code binary) ------------------
# Codebook: 1=No, 2=Yes, 3=No (trajectory), 4=Yes (trajectory),
#           9=Insufficient data, -1=Improbable value

recode_comorbidity <- function(x) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    x %in% c(1, 3)  ~ 0L,
    x %in% c(2, 4)  ~ 1L,
    x %in% c(9, -1) ~ NA_integer_,
    TRUE             ~ NA_integer_
  )
}

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    HBP    = recode_comorbidity(HBP),
    DIAB   = recode_comorbidity(DIAB),
    KID    = recode_comorbidity(KID),
    DYSLIP = recode_comorbidity(DYSLIP),
    LIV34  = recode_comorbidity(LIV34),
    FRP    = recode_comorbidity(FRP),
    FP     = recode_comorbidity(FP)
  )


# ---------- 2.2  BMI --------------------------------------------------------
# Codebook: 10.8–70.1 plausible; 999 = Insufficient data; -1 = Improbable

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    BMI = ifelse(BMI %in% c(999, -1), NA_real_, BMI),
    BMI = ifelse(!is.na(BMI) & (BMI < 10 | BMI > 80), NA_real_, BMI)
  )


# ---------- 2.3  CESD (Depression scale) ------------------------------------
# Codebook: 0–60; -1 = Missing

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    CESD      = ifelse(CESD == -1, NA_integer_, CESD),
    depressed = ifelse(!is.na(CESD), as.integer(CESD >= 16), NA_integer_)
  )


# ---------- 2.4  SMOKE — Binary per investigator ----------------------------
# Codebook: 1 = Never, 2 = Former, 3 = Current, Blank = Missing
# Collapse: Current smoker vs. Non/Former smoker

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    SMOKE_bin = case_when(
      SMOKE == 3          ~ "Current",
      SMOKE %in% c(1, 2)  ~ "Non/Former",
      TRUE                 ~ NA_character_
    ),
    SMOKE_bin = factor(SMOKE_bin, levels = c("Non/Former", "Current"))
  )


# ---------- 2.5  DKGRP (Alcohol use since last visit) -----------------------
# Codebook: 0=None, 1=1-3/wk, 2=4-13/wk, 3=>13/wk, Blank=Missing

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    DKGRP = factor(DKGRP,
                   levels = c(0, 1, 2, 3),
                   labels = c("None", "1-3/wk", "4-13/wk", ">13/wk"))
  )


# ---------- 2.6  HASHV (Marijuana use since last visit) ---------------------
# Codebook: 1 = No, 2 = Yes, Blank = Missing

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    HASHV = case_when(
      HASHV == 1 ~ 0L,
      HASHV == 2 ~ 1L,
      TRUE       ~ NA_integer_
    )
  )


# ---------- 2.7  HASHF (Marijuana frequency) --------------------------------

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    HASHF = factor(HASHF,
                   levels = c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4),
                   labels = c("Never", "Daily", "Weekly", "Monthly",
                              "Less often"),
                   ordered = TRUE)
  )


# ---------- 2.8  IDU (Injection drug use since last visit) ------------------
# Codebook: 1 = No, 2 = Yes, Blank = Missing

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    IDU = case_when(
      IDU == 1 ~ 0L,
      IDU == 2 ~ 1L,
      TRUE     ~ NA_integer_
    )
  )


# ---------- 2.9  HEROPIATE (Heroin/opiate use since last visit) -------------
# Codebook: 1 = No, 2 = Yes, -9 = Not specified, Blank = Missing

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    HEROPIATE = case_when(
      HEROPIATE == 1  ~ 0L,
      HEROPIATE == 2  ~ 1L,
      HEROPIATE == -9 ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE            ~ NA_integer_
    )
  )


# ---------- 2.10  RACE — Binary per investigator ----------------------------
# Non-Hispanic White vs. Other

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    RACE_bin = case_when(
      RACE == 1 ~ "Non-Hispanic White",
      RACE %in% c(2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8) ~ "Other",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    RACE_bin = factor(RACE_bin,
                      levels = c("Non-Hispanic White", "Other"))
  )


# ---------- 2.11  EDUCBAS — Binary per investigator -------------------------
# 4-year college degree or higher (5,6,7) vs. Below (1,2,3,4)

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    EDUC_bin = case_when(
      EDUCBAS %in% c(5, 6, 7) ~ "College degree+",
      EDUCBAS %in% c(1, 2, 3, 4) ~ "Below college degree",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    EDUC_bin = factor(EDUC_bin,
                      levels = c("Below college degree", "College degree+"))
  )


# ---------- 2.12  income ----------------------------------------------------
# Codebook: 1=<$10k ... 7=$60k+, 9=Refuse, Blank=Missing
# NOTE: Income is NOT used as a confounder per investigator/professor
#       guidance (education used for SES adjustment instead).
#       Retained in dataset for descriptive purposes only.

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    income = ifelse(income == 9, NA_real_, income),
    income_cat = case_when(
      income %in% c(1, 2)    ~ "<$20,000",
      income %in% c(3, 4)    ~ "$20,000-$39,999",
      income %in% c(5, 6, 7) ~ "$40,000+",
      TRUE                    ~ NA_character_
    ),
    income_cat = factor(income_cat,
                        levels = c("<$20,000",
                                   "$20,000-$39,999",
                                   "$40,000+"))
  )


# ---------- 2.13  ADH (ART adherence since last visit) ---------------------
# Codebook: 1=100%, 2=95-99%, 3=75-94%, 4=<75%, Blank=Missing

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    ADH_cat = case_when(
      ADH == 1 ~ "100%",
      ADH == 2 ~ "95-99%",
      ADH == 3 ~ "75-94%",
      ADH == 4 ~ "<75%",
      TRUE     ~ NA_character_
    ),
    ADH_cat = factor(ADH_cat,
                     levels = c("100%", "95-99%", "75-94%", "<75%"),
                     ordered = TRUE)
  )


# ---------- 2.14  hard_drugs (Primary exposure) -----------------------------
# Codebook: 0 = No, 1 = Yes, Blank = Missing

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    hard_drugs = factor(hard_drugs,
                        levels = c(0, 1),
                        labels = c("No", "Yes"))
  )


# ---------- 2.15  hivpos, ART, everART --------------------------------------

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    hivpos  = factor(hivpos,  levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Negative", "Positive")),
    ART     = factor(ART,     levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    everART = factor(everART, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes"))
  )


# =============================================================================
# 3.  DERIVED VARIABLES
# =============================================================================

# ---------- 3.1  Log10-transformed viral load --------------------------------
# VLOAD has NO exact zeros (minimum = 0.247), so no offset is needed.
# Using straight log10(VLOAD) avoids distorting the lower tail.

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    log_VLOAD = log10(VLOAD)
  )

# ---------- 3.2  Undetectable viral load flag --------------------------------
# Clinical threshold: < 50 copies/mL

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  mutate(
    VLOAD_undetectable = ifelse(!is.na(VLOAD),
                                as.integer(VLOAD < 50),
                                NA_integer_)
  )


# =============================================================================
# 4.  PLAUSIBLE-RANGE CHECKS
# =============================================================================

range_check <- hiv_clean %>%
  summarise(
    VLOAD_min    = min(VLOAD,    na.rm = TRUE),
    VLOAD_max    = max(VLOAD,    na.rm = TRUE),
    LEU3N_min    = min(LEU3N,    na.rm = TRUE),
    LEU3N_max    = max(LEU3N,    na.rm = TRUE),
    AGG_PHYS_min = min(AGG_PHYS, na.rm = TRUE),
    AGG_PHYS_max = max(AGG_PHYS, na.rm = TRUE),
    AGG_MENT_min = min(AGG_MENT, na.rm = TRUE),
    AGG_MENT_max = max(AGG_MENT, na.rm = TRUE),
    BMI_min      = min(BMI,      na.rm = TRUE),
    BMI_max      = max(BMI,      na.rm = TRUE),
    CESD_min     = min(CESD,     na.rm = TRUE),
    CESD_max     = max(CESD,     na.rm = TRUE),
    age_min      = min(age,      na.rm = TRUE),
    age_max      = max(age,      na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "value")

range_check


# =============================================================================
# 5.  IDENTIFY BASELINE HARD DRUG USE (PRIMARY EXPOSURE)
# =============================================================================

baseline_exposure <- hiv_clean %>%
  filter(years == 0) %>%
  select(newid, hard_drugs_bl = hard_drugs)

table(baseline_exposure$hard_drugs_bl, useNA = "always")

hiv_clean <- hiv_clean %>%
  left_join(baseline_exposure, by = "newid")


# =============================================================================
# 6.  CONSTRUCT ANALYTIC DATASETS
# =============================================================================

# ---------- 6.1  Baseline dataset (year 0) ----------------------------------
dat_bl <- hiv_clean %>%
  filter(years == 0)

# ---------- 6.2  Year 2 dataset ---------------------------------------------
dat_yr2 <- hiv_clean %>%
  filter(years == 2)

# ---------- 6.3  Merged analysis dataset ------------------------------------
baseline_covariates <- dat_bl %>%
  select(
    newid,
    # Exposure
    hard_drugs_bl,
    # Baseline outcomes
    VLOAD_bl      = VLOAD,
    log_VLOAD_bl  = log_VLOAD,
    LEU3N_bl      = LEU3N,
    AGG_PHYS_bl   = AGG_PHYS,
    AGG_MENT_bl   = AGG_MENT,
    # Confounders per investigator (age, race, education, BMI, smoking)
    age_bl        = age,
    RACE_bin_bl   = RACE_bin,
    EDUC_bin_bl   = EDUC_bin,
    BMI_bl        = BMI,
    SMOKE_bin_bl  = SMOKE_bin,
    # Additional descriptive variables (not in primary model)
    income_cat_bl = income_cat,
    DKGRP_bl      = DKGRP,
    HASHV_bl      = HASHV,
    CESD_bl       = CESD,
    depressed_bl  = depressed,
    HBP_bl        = HBP,
    DIAB_bl       = DIAB,
    LIV34_bl      = LIV34,
    KID_bl        = KID,
    DYSLIP_bl     = DYSLIP,
    FRP_bl        = FRP,
    FP_bl         = FP,
    TCHOL_bl      = TCHOL,
    TRIG_bl       = TRIG,
    LDL_bl        = LDL
  )

dat_analysis <- dat_yr2 %>%
  select(
    newid,
    # Outcomes at year 2
    VLOAD, log_VLOAD, VLOAD_undetectable,
    LEU3N,
    AGG_PHYS, AGG_MENT,
    # Adherence at year 2 (for mediation comparison)
    ADH_cat,
    # Time-varying
    age, BMI, CESD, ART
  ) %>%
  left_join(baseline_covariates, by = "newid")

# ---------- 6.4  Derive change scores ---------------------------------------
dat_analysis <- dat_analysis %>%
  mutate(
    delta_VLOAD     = VLOAD     - VLOAD_bl,
    delta_log_VLOAD = log_VLOAD - log_VLOAD_bl,
    delta_LEU3N     = LEU3N     - LEU3N_bl,
    delta_AGG_PHYS  = AGG_PHYS  - AGG_PHYS_bl,
    delta_AGG_MENT  = AGG_MENT  - AGG_MENT_bl
  )

# ---------- 6.5  Complete-case analytic sample (dat_cc) ---------------------
# Restrict to subjects complete on EVERY variable used in ANY model
# (all 4 outcomes, all 4 baseline outcomes, exposure, 5 confounders,
#  and the adherence mediator).  This ensures Table 1, frequentist
#  models, and Bayesian models all use the identical set of subjects.

cc_vars <- c(
  # Year 2 outcomes
  "log_VLOAD", "LEU3N", "AGG_PHYS", "AGG_MENT",
  # Baseline outcomes
  "log_VLOAD_bl", "LEU3N_bl", "AGG_PHYS_bl", "AGG_MENT_bl",
  # Exposure
  "hard_drugs_bl",
  # Confounders (investigator-specified)
  "age_bl", "RACE_bin_bl", "EDUC_bin_bl", "BMI_bl", "SMOKE_bin_bl",
  # Mediator
  "ADH_cat"
)

dat_cc <- dat_analysis %>%
  filter(complete.cases(across(all_of(cc_vars))))

message("Subjects in merged analysis set (BL + Y2):      ", nrow(dat_analysis))
message("Subjects in complete-case analytic sample:       ", nrow(dat_cc))
message("Dropped for any missing model variable:          ",
        nrow(dat_analysis) - nrow(dat_cc))
message("")
message("Exposure distribution in dat_cc:")
print(table(dat_cc$hard_drugs_bl, useNA = "always"))

# Verify zero missingness on all model variables
stopifnot(all(
  sapply(dat_cc[, cc_vars], function(x) sum(is.na(x))) == 0
))


# =============================================================================
# 7.  MISSING DATA SUMMARY
# =============================================================================

miss_bl <- dat_bl %>%
  summarise(across(everything(),
                   ~ sum(is.na(.)),
                   .names = "{.col}_n_miss")) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(),
               names_to  = "variable",
               values_to = "n_missing") %>%
  mutate(
    variable    = str_remove(variable, "_n_miss$"),
    pct_missing = round(100 * n_missing / nrow(dat_bl), 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_missing))

miss_bl

miss_yr2_outcomes <- dat_yr2 %>%
  summarise(
    VLOAD_miss    = sum(is.na(VLOAD)),
    LEU3N_miss    = sum(is.na(LEU3N)),
    AGG_PHYS_miss = sum(is.na(AGG_PHYS)),
    AGG_MENT_miss = sum(is.na(AGG_MENT))
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(),
               names_to  = "outcome",
               values_to = "n_missing") %>%
  mutate(
    outcome     = str_remove(outcome, "_miss$"),
    pct_missing = round(100 * n_missing / nrow(dat_yr2), 1)
  )

miss_yr2_outcomes


# =============================================================================
# 8.  ATTRITION ACCOUNTING
# =============================================================================

n_total_obs <- nrow(hiv_raw)
n_unique    <- length(unique(hiv_raw$newid))
n_baseline  <- nrow(dat_bl)
n_year2     <- nrow(dat_yr2)
n_both      <- length(intersect(dat_bl$newid, dat_yr2$newid))
n_lost      <- n_baseline - n_both

attrition_summary <- tibble(
  Stage = c("Total observations in raw data",
            "Unique subjects",
            "Subjects with baseline visit (year 0)",
            "Subjects with year 2 visit",
            "Subjects with both baseline and year 2",
            "Subjects lost to follow-up by year 2"),
  N     = c(n_total_obs, n_unique, n_baseline, n_year2, n_both, n_lost)
)

attrition_summary

attrition_by_drug <- dat_bl %>%
  mutate(retained_yr2 = as.integer(newid %in% dat_yr2$newid)) %>%
  group_by(hard_drugs) %>%
  summarise(
    n_baseline = n(),
    n_retained = sum(retained_yr2),
    n_lost     = n_baseline - n_retained,
    pct_lost   = round(100 * n_lost / n_baseline, 1),
    .groups    = "drop"
  )

attrition_by_drug


# =============================================================================
# 9.  FINAL VERIFICATION
# =============================================================================

# Confirm factor levels
levels(hiv_clean$RACE_bin)     # "Non-Hispanic White", "Other"
levels(hiv_clean$EDUC_bin)     # "Below college degree", "College degree+"
levels(hiv_clean$SMOKE_bin)    # "Non/Former", "Current"

# Confirm no zeros in VLOAD (validates no-offset decision)
sum(hiv_clean$VLOAD == 0, na.rm = TRUE)  # Should be 0
min(hiv_clean$VLOAD, na.rm = TRUE)       # Should be > 0

# Confirm no sentinel values in comorbidities
hiv_clean %>%
  select(HBP, DIAB, KID, DYSLIP, LIV34, FRP, FP) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ paste(sort(unique(na.omit(.))),
                                         collapse = ", ")))

# Dataset dimensions
dim(dat_bl)
dim(dat_yr2)
dim(dat_analysis)

head(dat_analysis, 5)
str(dat_analysis)


# =============================================================================
# 10.  SAVE CLEANED OBJECTS
# =============================================================================

save(hiv_clean, dat_bl, dat_yr2, dat_analysis, dat_cc,
     file = "hiv_cleaned.RData")
```




```{r}

###############################################################################
#                     TABLE 1 SCRIPT (kableExtra) — Complete Case
#
# Prereq: Run 01_data_cleaning_v3.R first to produce hiv_cleaned.RData
#
# Variables included reflect the analytic model:
#   Exposure:    hard_drugs_bl (baseline hard drug use)
#   Confounders: age, race (binary), education (binary), BMI, smoking (binary)
#   Mediator:    ADH_cat (adherence, categorical)
#   Outcomes:    Change from baseline to year 2 in —
#                  log10(VLOAD), LEU3N, AGG_PHYS, AGG_MENT
#
# Restricts to subjects with ALL 4 year-2 outcomes AND all 4 baseline
# outcomes observed (complete-case analytic sample).
###############################################################################

library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

load("hiv_cleaned.RData")


# =============================================================================
# 0.  DEFINE COMPLETE-CASE ANALYTIC SAMPLE & COMPUTE CHANGE SCORES
# =============================================================================
# dat_cc should already exist from the cleaning script. If re-running
# standalone, recreate it here:
# dat_cc <- dat_analysis %>%
#   filter(
#     !is.na(VLOAD),    !is.na(LEU3N),    !is.na(AGG_PHYS),    !is.na(AGG_MENT),
#     !is.na(VLOAD_bl), !is.na(LEU3N_bl), !is.na(AGG_PHYS_bl), !is.na(AGG_MENT_bl),
#     !is.na(hard_drugs_bl),
#     !is.na(age_bl), !is.na(RACE_bin_bl), !is.na(EDUC_bin_bl),
#     !is.na(BMI_bl), !is.na(SMOKE_bin_bl),
#     !is.na(ADH_cat)
#   )

# Compute change scores (Year 2 minus Baseline)
dat_cc <- dat_cc %>%
  mutate(
    chg_log_VLOAD = log_VLOAD - log_VLOAD_bl,
    chg_LEU3N     = LEU3N     - LEU3N_bl,
    chg_AGG_PHYS  = AGG_PHYS  - AGG_PHYS_bl,
    chg_AGG_MENT  = AGG_MENT  - AGG_MENT_bl
  )

# Verify no missing outcomes remain
sapply(dat_cc[, c("chg_log_VLOAD", "chg_LEU3N", "chg_AGG_PHYS", "chg_AGG_MENT")],
       function(x) sum(is.na(x)))

# Exposure group sizes in the complete-case sample
table(dat_cc$hard_drugs_bl, useNA = "always")


# =============================================================================
# 1.  HELPER FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

cont_row <- function(data, var, label, digits = 1) {
  by_group <- data %>%
    group_by(hard_drugs_bl) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_val = mean(.data[[var]], na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_val   = sd(.data[[var]], na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups  = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(cell = paste0(formatC(mean_val, format = "f", digits = digits),
                         " (",
                         formatC(sd_val, format = "f", digits = digits),
                         ")")) %>%
    select(hard_drugs_bl, cell) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = hard_drugs_bl, values_from = cell)

  overall <- data %>%
    summarise(
      mean_val = mean(.data[[var]], na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_val   = sd(.data[[var]], na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    mutate(cell = paste0(formatC(mean_val, format = "f", digits = digits),
                         " (",
                         formatC(sd_val, format = "f", digits = digits),
                         ")")) %>%
    pull(cell)

  tibble(Variable = label, Overall = overall, No = by_group$No, Yes = by_group$Yes)
}

cont_row_median <- function(data, var, label, digits = 1) {
  med_iqr <- function(x) {
    x <- x[!is.na(x)]
    paste0(formatC(median(x), format = "f", digits = digits),
           " [",
           formatC(quantile(x, 0.25), format = "f", digits = digits),
           ", ",
           formatC(quantile(x, 0.75), format = "f", digits = digits),
           "]")
  }

  by_group <- data %>%
    group_by(hard_drugs_bl) %>%
    summarise(cell = med_iqr(.data[[var]]), .groups = "drop") %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = hard_drugs_bl, values_from = cell)

  tibble(Variable = label, Overall = med_iqr(data[[var]]),
         No = by_group$No, Yes = by_group$Yes)
}

cat_rows <- function(data, var, label, level_labels = NULL) {
  data <- data %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[var]]))
  lvls <- if (!is.null(level_labels)) names(level_labels) else levels(factor(data[[var]]))

  rows <- map_dfr(lvls, function(lv) {
    display_label <- if (!is.null(level_labels)) level_labels[[lv]] else lv

    n_all   <- sum(data[[var]] == lv, na.rm = TRUE)
    pct_all <- 100 * n_all / nrow(data)
    overall_cell <- paste0(n_all, " (", formatC(pct_all, format = "f", digits = 1), ")")

    by_grp <- data %>%
      group_by(hard_drugs_bl) %>%
      summarise(
        n_lev = sum(.data[[var]] == lv, na.rm = TRUE),
        n_tot = n(),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      mutate(cell = paste0(n_lev, " (",
                           formatC(100 * n_lev / n_tot, format = "f", digits = 1),
                           ")")) %>%
      select(hard_drugs_bl, cell) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = hard_drugs_bl, values_from = cell)

    tibble(Variable = paste0("    ", display_label),
           Overall = overall_cell, No = by_grp$No, Yes = by_grp$Yes)
  })

  header <- tibble(Variable = label, Overall = "", No = "", Yes = "")
  bind_rows(header, rows)
}

binary_row <- function(data, var, label, yes_val = 1) {
  data <- data %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[var]]))

  n_all   <- sum(data[[var]] == yes_val, na.rm = TRUE)
  pct_all <- 100 * n_all / nrow(data)
  overall_cell <- paste0(n_all, " (", formatC(pct_all, format = "f", digits = 1), ")")

  by_grp <- data %>%
    group_by(hard_drugs_bl) %>%
    summarise(
      n_lev = sum(.data[[var]] == yes_val, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_tot = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(cell = paste0(n_lev, " (",
                         formatC(100 * n_lev / n_tot, format = "f", digits = 1),
                         ")")) %>%
    select(hard_drugs_bl, cell) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = hard_drugs_bl, values_from = cell)

  tibble(Variable = label, Overall = overall_cell, No = by_grp$No, Yes = by_grp$Yes)
}


# =============================================================================
# 2.  BUILD TABLE 1 FROM COMPLETE-CASE SAMPLE
# =============================================================================

n_total <- nrow(dat_cc)
n_no    <- sum(dat_cc$hard_drugs_bl == "No",  na.rm = TRUE)
n_yes   <- sum(dat_cc$hard_drugs_bl == "Yes", na.rm = TRUE)

n_row <- tibble(
  Variable = "n",
  Overall  = as.character(n_total),
  No       = as.character(n_no),
  Yes      = as.character(n_yes)
)

# --- Confounders (all baseline) ---
age_row  <- cont_row(dat_cc, "age_bl", "Age, mean (SD)")

race_row <- cat_rows(dat_cc, "RACE_bin_bl", "Race, n (%)",
                     level_labels = c("Non-Hispanic White" = "Non-Hispanic White",
                                      "Other" = "Other"))

educ_row <- cat_rows(dat_cc, "EDUC_bin_bl", "Education, n (%)",
                     level_labels = c("Below college degree" = "Below college degree",
                                      "College degree+" = "College degree+"))

bmi_row  <- cont_row(dat_cc, "BMI_bl", "BMI, mean (SD)")

smoke_row <- cat_rows(dat_cc, "SMOKE_bin_bl", "Smoking, n (%)",
                      level_labels = c("Non/Former" = "Non/Former",
                                       "Current" = "Current"))

# --- Mediator (adherence) ---
adh_row <- cat_rows(dat_cc, "ADH_cat", "ART Adherence, n (%)",
                    level_labels = c("100%"   = "100%",
                                     "95-99%" = "95-99%",
                                     "75-94%" = "75-94%",
                                     "<75%"   = "<75%"))

# --- Outcomes: Change from Baseline to Year 2 (Y2 - BL) ---
chg_logvload_row <- cont_row(dat_cc, "chg_log_VLOAD",
                             "\u0394 Log10(viral load), mean (SD)", digits = 2)
chg_leu3n_row    <- cont_row(dat_cc, "chg_LEU3N",
                             "\u0394 CD4+ T cell count (cells/\u00B5L), mean (SD)",
                             digits = 1)
chg_phys_row     <- cont_row(dat_cc, "chg_AGG_PHYS",
                             "\u0394 Physical QoL (AGG_PHYS), mean (SD)", digits = 1)
chg_ment_row     <- cont_row(dat_cc, "chg_AGG_MENT",
                             "\u0394 Mental QoL (AGG_MENT), mean (SD)", digits = 1)


# =============================================================================
# 3.  ASSEMBLE
# =============================================================================

table1_df <- bind_rows(
  n_row,
  age_row,
  race_row,
  educ_row,
  bmi_row,
  smoke_row,
  adh_row,
  chg_logvload_row,
  chg_leu3n_row,
  chg_phys_row,
  chg_ment_row
)

colnames(table1_df) <- c(
  "",
  paste0("Overall (N=", n_total, ")"),
  paste0("No Hard Drug Use (N=", n_no, ")"),
  paste0("Hard Drug Use (N=", n_yes, ")")
)


# =============================================================================
# 4.  RENDER AS HTML (kableExtra)
# =============================================================================

table1_kbl <- kbl(table1_df,
                  format    = "html",
                  align     = c("l", "c", "c", "c"),
                  caption   = paste0("Table 1. Baseline Characteristics and Outcome ",
                                     "Changes by Hard Drug Use (Complete-Case Sample)"),
                  escape    = FALSE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE,
                font_size  = 13,
                position   = "center") %>%
  pack_rows("Confounders", 2, 12, bold = TRUE, italic = TRUE,
            hline_before = TRUE) %>%
  pack_rows("Mediator", 13, 17, bold = TRUE, italic = TRUE,
            hline_before = TRUE) %>%
  pack_rows("Outcomes (Change from Baseline to Year 2)", 18, 21,
            bold = TRUE, italic = TRUE, hline_before = TRUE) %>%
  footnote(general = paste0(
             "Complete-case sample: restricted to subjects with all 4 outcomes ",
             "observed at both baseline and year 2, non-missing exposure and ",
             "confounders (N=", n_total, "). ",
             "Confounders (age, race, education, BMI, smoking) retained ",
             "regardless of significance per investigator specification. ",
             "Adherence is the sole mediator. ",
             "Outcome changes computed as Year 2 minus Baseline. ",
             "Continuous variables reported as mean (SD). ",
             "Categorical variables reported as n (%)."),
           general_title = "Note: ",
           footnote_as_chunk = TRUE)

table1_kbl

```
